effort_complexity.rationale: Wrap-don't-rewrite, but the loop-then-structured-verdict mechanism is new; provider quirks must stay contained.

design_decisions.choice: D1 — One InProcess struct, two registered identities: NewOAIChat() -> "oai-inprocess" (chat/completions family) and NewOAIResponses() -> "oai-responses-inprocess" (/v1/responses). Dispatch always re-resolves the concrete client from DispatchInput.ModelID via model.NewClient; the identities differ only in reported Name. Which prefixes route to which identity is S05's registry decision. AC-03's content-present test obligation is narrowed to the chat identity; the Responses identity is exempt as moot-by-construction (convertMessages emits tool-calling assistant turns as pure function_call input items — the Responses wire format structurally cannot express the tool-only content-drop).
options: one struct, two named identities, Dispatch re-resolves per ModelID (chosen)
two separate driver types duplicating the Dispatch logic
one registered identity with internal provider-prefix branching (loses the sworn#31 openai/ vs openai-completions/ routing split)
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), 2026-07-10, captain-proceed.md pins 3+4: D1 classified Type-1 and decided in the acknowledgement; AC-03 narrowed to the oai-inprocess chat identity by code inspection of internal/model/openai_responses.go convertMessages (no spec scope change required).
design_decisions.rationale: The registry (S05) and rewire (S06) consume the driver-identity model, making it a cross-slice contract; duplicating Dispatch would fork the error-mapping and metering logic the pins standardise.

design_decisions.choice: D5 — Cross-driver error contract: Dispatch returns the error chain carrying the underlying *model.Error (pin 1 option (a)) so the engine's model.IsTerminal terminal-halt keeps firing on the pre-S06 path; Result.ErrKind maps kind-for-kind through the shared driver.ErrKind* constants with KindAuth -> driver.ErrKindAuth as an explicit mapping (pin 2); agent.ErrMaxTurns wins first -> ErrKindTransient (retryable); on the verifier's structured-verdict call, a CLASSIFIED provider error (auth/credits/rate_limit/upstream/transient) keeps its real ErrKind and only otherwise-unclassified failures fold to ErrKindProtocol (pin 6). Rule-2 tracked obligation on S06-loop-dispatch-rewire: the rewired loop MUST treat Result.ErrKind in {auth, credits} as terminal to keep parity with the subprocess drivers.
options: (a) return the underlying *model.Error so model.IsTerminal keeps firing regardless of how S06 reads ErrKind (chosen) + tracked S06 obligation
(b) rely solely on the S06 rewire reading Result.ErrKind, collapsing terminal cases to "auth" like the subprocess drivers
fold every verdict-call failure into ErrKindProtocol (rejected: hides the fail-fast signal pin 1 protects)
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach), 2026-07-10, captain-proceed.md pins 1, 2 and 6: option (a) accepted with the tracked S06 obligation; ErrKind constants + explicit KindAuth mapping accepted; the pin-6 narrowing of AC-04's "structured-emission failure" explicitly blessed.
design_decisions.rationale: Removes the temporal coupling across the S06 rewire boundary: the intermediate state (S04 landed, S06 not yet) stays safe on its own; a transport auth failure is not a protocol failure and folding it would send triage down the wrong retry/escalation path.

design_decisions.choice: Placement — the driver lives in subpackage internal/driver/inprocess/ (package inprocess), not as internal/driver/inprocess.go, because ADR-0012 pins "internal/driver itself imports neither internal/model nor internal/agent" and TestNoWireImports enforces it over every file in that directory; the spec's literal touchpoint paths are unimplementable against its own AC-05 test command. Contract vocabulary is consumed via the exported driver.ErrKind* constants and driver.Result/DispatchInput types.
options: subpackage internal/driver/inprocess/ preserving the ADR-0012 leaf invariant (chosen)

same-directory placement + exempting inprocess*.go in imports_test.go (rejected: weakens a Coach-ratified Type-1 invariant, edits a file outside touchpoints, and makes every contract consumer transitively import the wire packages)
design_decisions.rationale: Forced move dictated by the existing Type-1 ADR-0012 decision, easy to reverse (git mv), narrow blast radius (S05 imports one package deeper; S08's touchpoint path note recorded in journal.md). Flagged prominently in journal.md and proof.json divergence for Coach/verifier visibility.

design_decisions.choice: D2 — Roles() declares implementer + verifier only (no captain: no AC references it). D3 — token/duration metering via an unexported chatMeter wrapping the client's Chat (pure observation; zero change to internal/agent). D4 — toModelMessages converts the agent transcript for the single ChatStructured verdict call; a final user nudge turn is appended so the conversation never ends on an assistant turn. D6 — CostUSD is the nominal ~$2/1M-token placeholder tagged CostSource="estimated" (captain-proceed.md pin 5: the contract's documented value, not a new "nominal") for S08 to replace. D7 — fail-closed guards: empty WorktreeRoot / AssertWorktree failure / chat-incapable client -> ErrKindConfig; verifier client without StructuredOutput -> ErrKindProtocol before the investigation loop spends tokens; empty Choices always checked, never indexed unchecked.
design_decisions.rationale: Narrow, local, reversible defaults acknowledged as sound in review.md (§2) and captain-proceed.md pin 4 (D6/D7 remain Type-2 defaults; D6's value fixed to "estimated" by pin 5).
